column2:
1. The more S-tier and A-tier case studies you give us, the faster and better your results will be. This is the #1 factor in campaign performance.
2. We don't use anything below B-tier in campaigns. C and D-tier case studies won't make it into your outreach -  don't waste too much time on weak ones unless it's a big name in the space.
3. Reference Tier Guide Example in Tab 5 along with examples with all sorts of offers we've ran in the past, and the tab next to this is an example of our most successful partner to show you what ""good"" looks like!
4. Unique mechanism is extremely important with competitive industries - let us know if you struggle to come up with them.

Any other questions let us know - we'll get back to you on slack within a few hours. We don't need a full breakdown of the case study > just key parts!
column20: TIER SCORING - Metrics for case studies

column21: Revenue, ideally revenue transformation or + $XX/month added etc.

column15: Any of the 2 above(i,e Transformational result/mechanism no logo OR Logo + transformation but no mechanism)
column21: Unique/rare results - e.g outranked a big competitor features on americans got talent, got acquired etc.
Revenue generating stats(Be specific!) - number of qualified leads, Top 1/3 rankings(Tell us the specific keywords), funding etc.
Markety stats but still important - CAC/CPA, ROAS, impressive views
Any % increases(percentages dont work), organic impressions etc.

column5: Sophia Block For Protein and Made by Mary how could you highlight something case study worthy? Candidly, from a macro level Made by Mary is tough, they are a shrinking brand that used to be propped up by organic demand and is highly commoditized, they're being crushed by competition continuing to enter a competitive market and rising acqusition costs with no retention but if you could highlight something... what would it be?
